countries/kuwait.summary: IRAN MISSILE/DRONE ATTACK ON US BASE JUN 28 — NEW (Day 1): IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones at US Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait. Kuwait intercepted two ballistic missiles; no casualties or damage reported at the base. Iran simultaneously struck Bahrain. Provoked by US airstrikes on five Iranian coastal sites. US and Iran subsequently agreed to stand down and hold Doha talks June 30. Desalination plants supplying 99% of Kuwait drinking water at continuing risk. Kuwait never assessed — this requires immediate assessment.

countries/bahrain.summary: IRAN MISSILE/DRONE ATTACK JUN 28 — NEW (Day 1): IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones at US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain. Residential building in Muharraq damaged near international airport. Bahrain air defenses intercepted multiple projectiles. Amnesty International published June 2026 research calling the strike pattern potential war crimes. Part of renewed US-Iran exchange after US struck five Iranian coastal sites. US and Iran subsequently agreed to stand down and hold Doha talks June 30. 28+ civilians killed across GCC from Iranian strikes since Feb 26 (cumulative). Bahrain has never been assessed — this requires immediate assessment.

countries/nigeria.summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK ONGOING (Jun 15-29): 35M+ at IPC Phase 3+ in lean season peak (highest ever recorded in Nigeria). 15,000+ at Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno State (Dikwa, Kaga, Kalabalge). FAO-WFP Hunger Hotspots: highest-concern global hotspot. 52.8M food insecure across West Africa/Sahel (Jun-Aug lean season). WFP funding crisis ongoing. No significant new development Jun 29 — situation at peak severity, continuing.

countries/lebanon.summary: FRAMEWORK AGREEMENT SIGNED JUN 28 — FRAGILE: US Secretary of State Rubio called the Jun 28 Washington deal "the beginning of the beginning." Israel resumed strikes two days later (Al Jazeera Jun 28/30). Hezbollah rejected framework as "humiliation, disgrace, surrender." Both sides presenting document as victory. 4,000+ killed in Lebanon since Mar 2, 2026. 1.2M+ displaced. Israeli military approved "continued operations in security zone." Framework includes Hezbollah disarmament — which Hezbollah calls "crosses all red lines." Ceasefire durability unclear. No new development Jun 29 beyond Jun 28 scan.

countries/venezuela.summary: EARTHQUAKE ONGOING (Day 5): 1,450 dead, 3,150 injured as of Jun 28 (updated from prior figures). UNICEF deployed additional staff, first air shipment arrived Jun 27 (20 MT medical/WASH supplies); second shipment of 48 MT due Jun 30 from Copenhagen. US OFAC General License 60 (Jun 25) authorizes earthquake relief transactions through Oct 23, 2026. US $150M committed through faith-based and UN channels. Survivors still being found in rubble Jun 28. Situation continues at crisis level. Government accountability concerns ongoing.

countries/qatar.summary: Gulf-states batch: Qatar's Al Udeid US Air Base targeted by Iran during Iran war. Desalination plants supply 99% of Qatar drinking water — struck earlier in conflict causing humanitarian concern. 70% of region food imports disrupted at peak. Doha talks scheduled June 30 between US/Iran envoys. Qatar never assessed.

fortune-500/unitedhealth-group.summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + CIVIL PROBE — ONGOING (Jun 15-29): Criminal and civil DOJ probes into Medicare Advantage overbilling, Optum Rx billing, antitrust. DOJ Jun 23 healthcare fraud takedown ($6.5B, 455 defendants) intensifies sector scrutiny. No new specific UHG development Jun 29. Probe ongoing at high severity.

countries/israel.summary: RESUMED LEBANON STRIKES JUN 28 + ONGOING GAZA (Jun 15-29): Israel resumed strikes on Lebanon June 28 despite framework agreement. UN Commission of Inquiry Jun 23 finding: genocide/atrocity crimes by deliberately targeting Palestinian children. 72,945+ Palestinians killed in Gaza war. Gaza ceasefire violations ongoing since Feb 28 ceasefire. ICC arrest warrants active. 4,000+ killed in Lebanon since Mar 2. No significant new development Jun 29 beyond Jun 28 scan. Continuing at critical severity.

countries/burkina-faso.summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK — ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 15-29): 52.8M food insecure across West Africa/Sahel. Burkina Faso hosts largest IDP population after Nigeria. JNIM attacks continue; besieged population 3.5M+. 28% of $3.7B regional appeal funded. No new major development Jun 29 — situation ongoing at peak severity. Continuation of Jun 28 scan status.

countries/democratic-republic-of-c.summary: EBOLA THIRD-LARGEST OUTBREAK EVER — ONGOING: As of Jun 25, 1,155 confirmed cases / 304 deaths / 385 hospitalized. Bundibugyo species (no vaccine/treatment). Growing faster than any prior outbreak in recorded history. Ituri province: 1,054 cases. ECDC update expected Jun 29 (not yet published at scan time). HRW Jun 17: "Trump Declared Peace in Congo. This Is the Reality" — M23/Rwanda atrocities ongoing. 5.8M internally displaced. Sexual violence used as weapon of war. No new case count update for Jun 29 specifically — next update pending.

countries/saudi-arabia.summary: Gulf-states batch: Saudi Arabia targeted by Iranian drone/missile strikes since Feb 26 (38+ missiles, 435+ drones through mid-March). Amnesty International June 2026 research covers war crimes investigation into Iran strikes on GCC states. Saudi Arabia last assessed Apr 20 — assessment should incorporate ongoing Iran war civilian impact.

countries/iran.summary: US-IRAN CEASEFIRE NEAR-COLLAPSE JUN 28-29 — NEW ESCALATION: Iran attacked Bahrain and Kuwait with missiles and drones on Jun 28, after US struck five Iranian coastal sites. Iran threatened "complete halt" in negotiations. Trump warned Iran "will no longer exist." Both sides subsequently agreed to stand down temporarily; Doha talks scheduled Tuesday Jun 30. Islamabad Memorandum (Jun 17 ceasefire) survived its closest brush with full collapse. Iran execution surge continues: 784+ YTD (37-year high per NCRI Jun 28 brief). Hunger strikes Week 126+ across 56 prisons. All developments within 14-day window.

countries/yemen.summary: WFP STAFF TERMINATION ONGOING — US-IRAN CEASEFIRE EFFECTS (Jun 15-29): WFP terminated all 365 staff contracts in Houthi northern Yemen. 73 UN workers detained. 22.3M need assistance. Only 12.7% of $2.16B appeal funded. Islamabad Memorandum near-collapse Jun 28 (Strait of Hormuz reopened) could affect delivery costs. Doha talks Jun 30. 18.3M acutely food insecure. No new development Jun 29. Continuation of prior critical status.

countries/sudan.summary: SAF REJECTS CEASEFIRE, OPERATIONS CONTINUE (Jun 29): Al-Burhan stated SAF will operate "with the precision of digging with a needle" until RSF is fully dismantled — rejecting RSF acceptance of Quad humanitarian ceasefire. El Obeid: 500,000+ civilians besieged; UN Under-Secretary DiCarlo Jun 27 said window "rapidly narrowing." Drone strikes on civilian infrastructure continue. Famine confirmed in El-Fasher and Kadugli. US State Dept: "imminent risk of mass atrocities." No new major military development Jun 29 beyond Jun 28 scan. Ongoing at critical severity.

countries/south-sudan.summary: FAMINE RISK ONGOING (Jun 15-29): 7.8M face acute food insecurity (56% of population). 2.2M children acutely malnourished. Four counties at famine risk. 140,000 displaced in Akobo alone. WFP serving 1.5M fewer than planned. No significant new development Jun 29. Continuation of prior critical status.

countries/somalia.summary: IPC FAMINE DECLARATION STATUS UNCONFIRMED — THRESHOLD PASSED: End-of-June IPC famine threshold for Buur Hakaba district has now passed. GAM rate 37.1% (IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe). Most malnutrition health facilities closed. WFP reaching approximately 1-in-10 people. No confirmed formal IPC/FSNAU famine declaration published as of Jun 29. Oxfam and Action Against Hunger issued statements warning of famine emergence. 6M people (1-in-3 of analyzed population) at Crisis/worse. No material new development Jun 29 — formal declaration status urgently needs verification.

Belarus removed drone-navigation transponders but Russian drones still detected in Belarusian airspace Jun 15-21. War ongoing.

countries/bolivia.summary: STATE OF EMERGENCY RESOLVED JUN 23 — AFTERMATH (Jun 19-29): Bolivia declared state of emergency Jun 19-20 restricting mass gatherings during nationwide protests by miners, teachers, farmers. 17 killed, 365 arrested. All blockades lifted Jun 23. COB (workers center) reached agreement Jun 19. State of emergency remains in effect for 90 days (restricts assembly). Economic crisis: worst in decades. Bolivia was last assessed Jun 24 — assessment should incorporate state of emergency events.

countries/mali.summary: Africa-Sahel batch: 1.5M Malians expected at Crisis+ in lean season. Areas with reduced food rations saw 64% surge in acute hunger. 52.8M food insecure across Sahel lean season (Jun-Aug). No specific new development Jun 29.

countries/russia.summary: Europe-conflict batch: Russia-Ukraine war ongoing. Russia Matters war report card Jun 24 (within window) documents ongoing conflict dynamics. No ceasefire. Russian offensive campaign continues.

countries/myanmar.summary: UN OHCHR REPORT JUN 22 — HUMANITARIAN CRISIS DEEPENS: UN human rights office published report June 22 documenting how foreign aid decline is compounding suffering from ongoing military attacks. 702+ verified civilian deaths documented (Aug 2025-Jan 2026), 476 from airstrikes. 16.2M people (1/3 of population) need humanitarian assistance including 5M children. Aid cuts forced program closures, civil society layoffs, closure of women's safe houses and health centers. 2026 appeal only 26% funded ($234M of $890M). Military blockades degrading emergency healthcare. AI-powered surveillance cited in ongoing civilian targeting.
